ex_en: Temporal success is fleeting; character is eternal.
ex_zh: 世俗的成功是短暂的；品格是永恒的。

quote_en: The politician tried to temporize rather than answer the question.
quote_zh: 那位政客试图拖延而不是回答问题。
ex_en: To temporize is to lose the moment; decide, even if imperfectly.
ex_zh: 拖延就是失去时机；做出决定，即使不完美。

quote_en: Honesty is a basic tenet of their religion.
quote_zh: 诚实是他们宗教的基本信条。
ex_en: A tenet not tested by life is just a slogan; live your beliefs.
ex_zh: 一个未经生活考验的信条只是口号；活出你的信念。

quote_en: The idea is theoretical and has not been tested.
quote_zh: 这个想法是理论性的，尚未经过测试。
ex_en: Theoretical knowledge is a map; practical wisdom is the journey.
ex_zh: 理论知识是一张地图；实践智慧是旅程。

def_en: Demanding complete obedience to a single party
quote_en: The totalitarian regime banned all opposition.
quote_zh: 那个极权主义政权禁止一切反对派。
ex_en: A totalitarian mind — one that brooks no dissent — is a prison for the soul.
ex_zh: 一个不允许任何异议的极权主义头脑是灵魂的监狱。

quote_en: The radio tower transmits signals to distant receivers.
quote_zh: 广播塔向远处的接收器传输信号。
ex_en: Kindness is transmitted not by words, but by the look in your eyes when you listen.
ex_zh: 善意不是通过言语传播的，而是通过你倾听时眼中的神情。

quote_en: The explorer traversed the desert on camelback.
quote_zh: 探险家骑着骆驼穿越了沙漠。
ex_en: To traverse a difficulty is to earn the right to help others across the same.
ex_zh: 穿越一个困难就是赢得了帮助他人穿越同一个困难的权利。

quote_en: The family trekked through the mountains for three days.
quote_zh: 那家人在山里长途跋涉了三天。
ex_en: Every great achievement begins as a trek with no visible destination.
ex_zh: 每一项伟大的成就都始于一次没有可见目的地的长途跋涉。

quote_en: The advice was trite: "Follow your dreams" without any real guidance.
quote_zh: 这条建议很陈腐：“追随你的梦想”，没有任何真正的指导。
ex_en: A trite saying may still be true; do not dismiss it just because you have heard it before.
ex_zh: 一句陈腐的话可能仍然是真实的；不要仅仅因为你以前听过就 dismiss 它。

quote_en: The cat was uninterested in the new toy.
quote_zh: 那只猫对新玩具不感兴趣。
ex_en: To be uninterested in the suffering of others is to be less than human.
ex_zh: 对他人的痛苦不感兴趣就是不如人。

quote_en: The unremitting rain caused flooding.
quote_zh: 不停歇的雨造成了洪水。
ex_en: Unremitting kindness, even when not returned, is never wasted.
ex_zh: 不间断的善意，即使没有得到回报，也永远不会浪费。

quote_en: The general tried to usurp the throne.
quote_zh: 将军试图篡夺王位。
ex_en: Do not let fear usurp the throne of your courage; you are the rightful ruler.
ex_zh: 不要让恐惧篡夺你勇气的王位；你才是合法的统治者。

def_en: The illegal practice of lending at high interest
quote_en: The loan shark was arrested for usury.
quote_zh: 高利贷者因放高利贷被捕。
ex_en: Usury of the soul — taking more emotional credit than you have earned — is a debt you cannot repay.
ex_zh: 灵魂的高利贷——索取比你应得更多的情感信用——是一笔你无法偿还的债务。

quote_en: The experiment validated the scientist's hypothesis.
quote_zh: 实验证实了科学家的假设。
ex_en: You do not need others to validate your worth; it was given at birth and cannot be revoked.
ex_zh: 你不需要别人来确认你的价值；它是在出生时赋予的，无法被撤销。

quote_en: The hero vanquished the dragon and saved the kingdom.
quote_zh: 英雄征服了巨龙，拯救了王国。
ex_en: The only enemy you must vanquish is the one who whispers that you cannot.
ex_zh: 你必须征服的唯一敌人是那个低语说你不能的声音。

quote_en: The senator was a vehement opponent of the new law.
quote_zh: 那位参议员是该新法律的激烈反对者。
ex_en: A vehement belief held without evidence is a shout in an empty room.
ex_zh: 没有证据的激烈信念是空房间里的一声喊叫。

quote_en: The verbosity of the report made it hard to read.
quote_zh: 报告的冗长使其难以阅读。
ex_en: Verbosity is the enemy of clarity; say what you mean, then stop.
ex_zh: 冗长是清晰的敌人；说出你的意思，然后停止。

def_en: Able to adapt to many functions
quote_en: The versatile actor could play comedy and drama equally well.
quote_zh: 那位多才多艺的演员能同样出色地扮演喜剧和戏剧。
ex_en: A versatile mind can find a way in any situation; rigidity is the real handicap.
ex_zh: 一个多才多艺的头脑在任何情况下都能找到出路；僵化才是真正的障碍。

quote_en: There are several restaurants in the vicinity of the hotel.
quote_zh: 酒店附近有几家餐馆。
ex_en: The vicinity of a kind person makes the whole neighborhood safer.
ex_zh: 一个善良的人附近使整个社区更安全。

def_en: To hold and use a weapon or tool
quote_en: The knight wielded his sword with great skill.
quote_zh: 骑士熟练地挥舞着他的剑。
ex_en: Wield your words as carefully as a scalpel; they can heal or cut.
ex_zh: 像使用手术刀一样小心地使用你的话语；它们可以治愈或切割。

quote_en: The rise of xenophobia led to discriminatory laws.
quote_zh: 仇外情绪的上升导致了歧视性法律。
ex_en: Xenophobia is a fear of the unknown; cure it by getting to know the unknown.
ex_zh: 仇外是对未知的恐惧；通过了解未知来治愈它。
